dataStatusNote: Category totals are placeholders derived from the November 2025 budget news release (Protective Services 31%, total operating $236M). Replace with exact line items transcribed from the budget PDF before launch.

categories.name: Protective Services
categories.description: Codiac RCMP, Moncton Fire Department, bylaw enforcement, emergency preparedness.

categories.name: Transportation Services
categories.description: Road maintenance, snow & ice control, public transit (Codiac Transpo), traffic signals, sidewalks.

categories.name: Recreation & Cultural Services
categories.description: Parks, arenas, pools, the Avenir Centre, public library, festivals & events, community grants.

categories.name: General Government
categories.description: Mayor & Council, City Manager's office, finance, HR, IT, legal, clerk, communications.

categories.name: Debt Servicing
categories.description: Interest and principal payments on existing long-term debt used to finance past capital projects (recreation facilities, infrastructure, etc.).
categories.footnote: Servicing yesterday's borrowed capital — the cost of past infrastructure investments paid off over decades.

categories.name: Transfer to Capital Reserve
categories.description: Operating dollars moved into reserves to pay for future infrastructure: new roads, recreation facilities, equipment, vehicles. The City's primary 'pay-as-you-go' way to fund capital projects without borrowing.
categories.footnote: This is how your property tax helps fund the City's separate $92M annual capital budget. The rest of the capital budget comes from debt, provincial/federal grants, and developer charges.

categories.name: Environmental Health
categories.description: Solid waste collection, recycling, street cleaning, environmental services.

categories.name: Environmental Development
categories.description: Urban planning, building inspection, economic development, tourism, downtown revitalization.

categories.name: Public Health
categories.description: Public-health-related expenditures and transfers (e.g. social-housing partnerships, regional health programs).
